rules.statement: RETIRED (#820 Group A, superseded by capability.taxonomy.roles_require_canonical_capabilities and capability.taxonomy.resources_provide_canonical_capabilities). This rule no longer dispatches to a mapping. Historical marker only — see rationale.
rules.rationale: Confirmed via live diagnostic after #820 Group A implemented capability_taxonomy_whitelist in KnowledgeGateEngine (eede781d): this rule and capability.taxonomy.no_ad_hoc_capabilities mapped to the identical dispatch (engine=knowledge_gate, check_type=capability_taxonomy_whitelist), the same database_sources (core.cognitive_roles.required_capabilities, core.llm_resources.provided_capabilities), and the same artifact_sources globs (.intent/workers/**/*, .intent/workflows/**/*) — measured at 58 files, zero capability-bearing keys, contributing nothing. Both produced the identical 6 findings against live data. capability.taxonomy.roles_require_canonical_capabilities and capability.taxonomy.resources_provide_canonical_capabilities already express the two real governed surfaces precisely, one database_sources entry each; this rule added no coverage beyond theirs. Retired rather than deleted so the prior declaration stays traceable. Its mapping entry and remediation-map entry are removed; the two precise rules remain blocking and continue producing all 6 real findings (4 role, 2 resource).

rules.statement: RETIRED (#820 Group A, superseded by capability.taxonomy.roles_require_canonical_capabilities and capability.taxonomy.resources_provide_canonical_capabilities). This rule no longer dispatches to a mapping. Historical marker only — see rationale.
rules.rationale: Confirmed via live diagnostic after #820 Group A implemented capability_taxonomy_whitelist in KnowledgeGateEngine (eede781d): this rule and capability.taxonomy.canonical_only mapped to the identical dispatch (engine=knowledge_gate, check_type=capability_taxonomy_whitelist) and the same database_sources and artifact_sources, producing the identical 6 findings against live data. Its only distinguishing mapping param, reject_unknown: true, was never read by the implementation and did not differentiate its behavior from canonical_only — the two were byte-identical in practice, not merely in intent. capability.taxonomy.roles_require_canonical_capabilities and capability.taxonomy.resources_provide_canonical_capabilities already express the two real governed surfaces precisely; this rule added no coverage beyond theirs. Retired rather than deleted so the prior declaration stays traceable. Its mapping entry and remediation-map entry are removed; the two precise rules remain blocking and continue producing all 6 real findings (4 role, 2 resource).

rules.statement: Capability names MUST remain model-neutral and provider-neutral. Capabilities MUST describe technical ability, not vendor, model, or implementation identity.
Cognitive roles MAY require one or more capabilities, but every required capability MUST be canonical.
LLM resources and other cognitive execution resources MAY declare one or more provided capabilities, but every provided capability MUST be canonical.
